activity.summary: person, moment, core_job, consequence, primary_outcome, primary_action, platform, stack, representative_content, constraints, character, known_facts, assumptions

For hiring reviewers in a fast portfolio scan, privilege credible proof and explicit project status over polished outcome language so Drew's actual seniority is legible without overstating unverified results

commitment.optimize_for: Credibility, scanability, and senior decision evidence
commitment.thesis: For hiring reviewers in a fast portfolio scan, privilege credible proof and explicit project status over polished outcome language so Drew's actual seniority is legible without overstating unverified results

directions.bet: Small systemic content and hierarchy changes can repair credibility without changing the portfolio's identity
directions.failure_case: The evidence labels may feel defensive or resume-like if they overwhelm the story
directions.falsification: Reviewers still cannot distinguish shipped, concept, and unvalidated work after a 60-second scan
directions.hierarchy: Measured impact first, selected case studies second; within cases, status and strongest proof before narrative and imagery
directions.interaction: Reviewers scan quantified impact, choose a case, follow decisions with adjacent artifacts, then continue or contact
directions.name: Evidence ledger
directions.organizing_idea: Keep the editorial homepage and case-study model, but make evidence level the repeating information spine: measured impact on the homepage, status and proof in each case, decisions paired with artifacts, and an honest end-state
directions.selection_rationale: It fixes the audited credibility failure while preserving the portfolio's strongest asset: a restrained, coherent editorial identity. The flagship direction overweights an unmeasured case, while the taxonomy adds more categorization than this small corpus needs.
directions.tradeoffs: Does not provide full narrative depth for older quantified work and keeps three current projects in the corpus

directions.bet: One deep senior-service-design story creates a clearer level signal than three equal-weight cases
directions.failure_case: The strongest visual story still rests on concept work and the overclaim becomes more prominent
directions.falsification: Reviewers ask for proof that the portal shipped or that onboarding improved
directions.hierarchy: FORGE first and visually dominant; Velveteen and SBIR secondary
directions.interaction: Reviewers enter the flagship directly, then inspect builder breadth if interested
directions.name: Single flagship
directions.organizing_idea: Make FORGE the dominant long-form case and reduce other work to short supporting entries
directions.tradeoffs: Concentrates the portfolio around a case whose shipped outcome is not yet demonstrated

directions.bet: Explicit categories create maximum honesty and make a nine-year career legible
directions.failure_case: The taxonomy becomes the visual subject and the projects feel secondary
directions.falsification: Reviewers spend more time interpreting categories than evaluating Drew's work
directions.hierarchy: Proven impact, shipped work, then experiments
directions.interaction: Reviewers choose by evidence type rather than a single work list
directions.name: Work taxonomy
directions.organizing_idea: Split the homepage into proven impact, shipped products, and experiments, with different page treatments for each
directions.tradeoffs: Adds more homepage structure and risks making a small body of work feel fragmented

fingerprint.color_role: Near-black and warm neutrals create an archival field; rose marks orientation and provenance, while higher-contrast muted text preserves secondary hierarchy without sacrificing legibility
fingerprint.composition_rule: Quantified proof occupies a stable leading column; each case moves from status and claim into decisions, with the artifact placed beside or immediately after the decision it supports
fingerprint.imagery_icon_language: Only project artifacts and product screens are used; images document decisions, use honest captions, preserve aspect ratio, and provide descriptive alternatives
fingerprint.material_model: A continuous case-file publication separated by rules and artifact strips, not a dashboard or card collection
fingerprint.signature_behavior: Restrained content reveal preserves reading cadence, while case-to-case navigation keeps the portfolio moving without decorative motion
fingerprint.type_voice: Documentary and editorial: Lora carries reflective claims, Geist carries readable narrative, and Geist Mono labels status, provenance, and numbers

interaction_model.decision_points: Reviewers choose between proven career impact and current case studies, then between live builder work, organizational service design, and an experiment still under validation. Labels make evidence level explicit before commitment.
interaction_model.entry_orientation: The homepage opens with Drew's outcomes-first thesis, immediately followed by quantified career impact and then selected current work. Each case card states outcome or capability honestly.
interaction_model.feedback: Existing hover, focus, and Reveal behavior remains; links have visible focus, external links remain explicit, and reduced-motion preferences suppress transitions.
interaction_model.information_disclosure: Always visible: project status, role, outcome/capability, and strongest proof. In context: narrative decisions and visuals. Deferred: secondary artifacts and implementation detail. Never conceal validation gaps behind visual polish.
interaction_model.navigation_model: Homepage anchors remain stable; case pages retain back navigation and add next-case, resume, and email actions at the end. Static routes preserve direct links and browser navigation.
interaction_model.primary_flow: Scan identity and quantified impact; choose a current case; read status, role, scope, and strongest proof; follow two or three key decisions with adjacent artifacts; review what changed and what remains unproven; continue to the next case or contact Drew.
interaction_model.recovery_continuity: 404 route returns home; every case ends with a path to another case or contact; missing public links do not render dead controls; image alt text preserves the evidence description if media fails.

product_truth.evidence_source: Repository content, rendered portfolio, and resume claims supplied in the project
product_truth.residual_risk: No hiring-manager usability study, analytics, or independent verification of project outcome claims is available

project.name: Drew McFarland portfolio credibility pass
project.source_prompt: Great - make these changes then push to git.

refusals.rationale: A fabricated or thin case would undermine the credibility problem this pass is meant to solve
refusals.refusal: Do not invent a full Kessel Run case study without source artifacts or project-specific narrative

refusals.rationale: Reviewers need to distinguish what exists from what has been validated
refusals.refusal: Do not give concepts, shipped capabilities, and measured outcomes equal evidentiary weight

refusals.protects: The portfolio's established identity and reading focus
refusals.rationale: The current visual language is coherent; the deficit is content hierarchy and evidence
refusals.refusal: Do not replace the restrained editorial system with cards, ornamental dashboards, or decorative motion

situation.assumptions: Hiring reviewers value honest evidence levels and senior decision-making more than a standard process narrative
situation.character: Editorial, documentary, and candid: restrained visuals, concrete evidence, explicit uncertainty
situation.consequence: Unsupported claims make the portfolio's outcomes-first thesis feel promotional; specific status and proof make it trustworthy
situation.constraints: Preserve the dark tactile artifact system; do not invent metrics or validation; maintain public-safe defense details; no new dependencies
situation.core_job: Judge Drew's level, individual contribution, ability to navigate complexity, and record of changing user or organizational outcomes
situation.known_facts: The resume contains 300% DAU growth, 20% faster maintenance initiation, roughly 40% platform adoption growth, and standards adopted across five programs. FORGE includes five teams and a seventeen-stage onboarding map. Velveteen is live. SBIR Radar has no public link or portfolio evidence of validation.
situation.moment: A one-to-three-minute initial scan, usually before an interview, with deeper reading only if the first evidence is credible
situation.person: Design leaders, recruiters, and hiring managers evaluating senior product design and design leadership candidates
situation.platform: Responsive web, desktop-first hiring review with complete mobile adaptation
situation.primary_action: Choose a case study, understand its evidence, then continue to the next case or contact Drew
situation.primary_outcome: Within the homepage and first case-study viewport, the reviewer can distinguish measured impact, shipped capability, concept work, and validation still in progress
situation.representative_content: FORGE service-design work, Velveteen builder work, SBIR Radar experiment, and three quantified user/conversion outcomes from the resume
situation.stack: Next.js 16 App Router server components, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4

states.accessibility: Semantic hierarchy, skip link, responsive reflow
states.available_information: Identity, quantified impact, project status, and selected work
states.behavior: Server-rendered homepage presents evidence before current work
states.content: Real quantified resume impact and three current projects
states.name: first-use
states.recovery: Resume and email remain directly available

states.accessibility: Focus and link labels remain consistent
states.available_information: Stable anchors, work ordering, and case navigation
states.behavior: No onboarding overlay or transient state; browser history and anchors remain predictable
states.name: returning-use
states.user_goal: Resume evaluation or open another case

states.available_information: Server-rendered text arrives independently; image dimensions reserve space
states.behavior: Static pages render on the server; Next Image reserves artifact aspect ratios and lazy-loads below-fold media
states.name: loading
states.recovery: Alt text and captions preserve meaning if imagery is delayed
states.trigger: Initial document or artifact image is loading

states.name: empty-unconfigured
states.reason: No user-configured data or collection exists

states.name: no-results
states.reason: The portfolio has no search or filter flow

states.name: partial-stale-conflicting
states.reason: Published case-study content is static; uncertainty is expressed as project status and evidence labels rather than data freshness

states.name: permission-restriction
states.reason: All portfolio routes are public and require no permissions

states.name: invalid-input
states.reason: The portfolio contains no input forms

states.accessibility: Semantic heading, native button, visible focus, no error-code burden
states.available_information: A plain-language failure message and retry action
states.behavior: Global error boundary renders a branded retry screen
states.content: Something went wrong; try again; return home
states.name: recoverable-error
states.recovery: Retry button invokes reset; home link provides escape

states.available_information: Server-rendered text, reserved media space, explicit link destinations
states.behavior: No data-dependent client flow; page content remains meaningful without completed image loads
states.name: offline-slow-timeout
states.recovery: Browser retry/back and direct route links
states.trigger: Slow connection delays images or navigation
states.user_goal: Keep orientation and read available content

states.accessibility: Document order stays logical without sticky positioning
states.available_information: Stable URL, section order, sticky identity block, and case footer
states.behavior: No volatile local state; browser scroll restoration is left to platform behavior
states.name: interrupted-resumed
states.primary_action: Continue reading or select next case
states.trigger: Reviewer leaves a case and returns with browser navigation

states.name: destructive-undo
states.reason: No destructive or mutating actions exist

states.accessibility: Descriptive link text and touch targets
states.available_information: Next case, resume, and email contact
states.behavior: A case footer replaces the previous dead end
states.content: Next case title plus contact actions
states.name: success-partial-success
states.recovery: Back to selected work remains available
states.trigger: Reviewer reaches the end of a case

states.accessibility: Supports browser zoom and text wrapping; no uppercase-only long labels
states.available_information: All fields wrap in a single-column priority order at narrow widths
states.behavior: Readable max-widths, wrapping grid tracks, and no fixed-height text containers
states.content: FORGE outcome and long role names
states.name: long-localized-content
states.trigger: Long project titles, proof statements, or translated browser text sizing
states.user_goal: Read without clipping or horizontal overflow

states.accessibility: Visible focus, semantic reading order, color-independent statuses
states.behavior: Responsive grids stack; Reveal disables entrance animation; global transitions collapse
states.name: large-text-reduced-motion
states.trigger: Browser zoom, large text, or prefers-reduced-motion
states.user_goal: Access the complete portfolio without clipping or unwanted movement

states.accessibility: Semantic headings, definition lists, links, visible focus, and single-column mobile reflow
states.available_information: Role, background, three quantified user/conversion outcomes, selected projects, project evidence level
states.behavior: Quantified user and adoption impact precedes the project list; current projects use honest outcome or capability language
states.content: 300% DAU growth, 20% faster maintenance initiation, roughly 40% platform adoption growth
states.name: homepage-first-scan
states.recovery: Resume and email remain available without opening a case
states.trigger: A reviewer opens the portfolio root
states.user_goal: Understand Drew's level and decide whether the work merits a deeper read

states.accessibility: H1, labelled metadata, figures with alt text and captions, readable measure
states.available_information: Status, role, scope, evidence statement, narrative decisions, artifacts
states.behavior: Short defensible outcome leads; proof strip separates known change from unmeasured aspiration
states.content: FORGE: five teams, seventeen stages, shared operating picture; no unsupported onboarding-time reduction
states.name: measured-case-entry
states.recovery: Back to work and next-case links
states.trigger: A reviewer opens a case with observed organizational or user evidence
states.user_goal: Verify what Drew changed and how

states.accessibility: External links are named, focusable, and accompanied by visible labels
states.available_information: Live status, public links, seven-stage pipeline, current validation gap
states.behavior: Capability language replaces outcome language; live and under-validation states are both explicit
states.content: Live product; four security tools; explanation quality remains under evaluation
states.name: live-capability-case
states.primary_action: Inspect the security-review decision and live product
states.recovery: Return, visit product, continue to next case, or contact
states.user_goal: Assess Drew's builder range without mistaking a live capability for validated adoption

states.accessibility: No empty link group is rendered; status is textual, not color-only
states.available_information: Prototype status, target triage job, pipeline risk, screenshots, no public link
states.behavior: The page says validation in progress and avoids measured speed claims
states.content: Prototype; six decision signals; no portfolio evidence of task-time improvement
states.name: experiment-case
states.primary_action: Evaluate the decision and validation plan
states.recovery: Next-case and contact links remain available despite no product URL
states.user_goal: Understand the product bet and what is not yet validated

states.accessibility: Touch targets remain at least 44px where interactive; text does not rely on hover
states.available_information: Same status, proof, content, and actions in linear priority order
states.behavior: Asymmetric desktop grids collapse to one column; proof rows and impact entries stack
states.content: Longest FORGE copy and multi-line impact labels
states.name: small-viewport
states.primary_action: Open or continue reading a case
states.recovery: Browser back and explicit case navigation remain visible
states.trigger: Viewport below the desktop grid breakpoint
states.user_goal: Scan and read without horizontal overflow or lost hierarchy

states.available_information: All content and hierarchy without animation
states.behavior: Existing global media query reduces transitions and Reveal respects the preference
states.name: reduced-motion

states.accessibility: Descriptive alternative text; captions do not duplicate only decorative detail
states.available_information: Figure label, descriptive alt text, and explanatory caption
states.behavior: Reserved image dimensions limit layout shift and text remains meaningful
states.content: Service blueprint, event storm, product screens
states.name: missing-or-failed-image
states.recovery: No blocked flow; raw image link is optional
states.trigger: An artifact image loads slowly or fails

states.name: authentication-and-permissions
states.reason: The portfolio is public and contains no authenticated actions

states.name: destructive-or-data-submission
states.reason: The portfolio has no destructive actions or forms; email uses a mailto link

verification.observed: npm run lint exited 0; npm run build compiled successfully, passed TypeScript, and generated all 8 static pages including all three work routes.

verification.method: In-app browser screenshots and DOM snapshots
verification.observed: Homepage renders quantified impact before current work; FORGE opening exposes status, scope, and strongest proof; artifacts render immediately after their supporting narrative; case footer exposes next case, resume, email, and all-work paths.

verification.method: In-app browser viewport override and DOM measurement
verification.observed: Layouts collapse to one column, project status remains in the identity block, horizontal artifact strips stay contained, and document scrollWidth equals viewport width at both 390px and 320px.

verification.artifact: Homepage to case study to continuation footer
verification.method: Semantic DOM inspection and primary-flow walkthrough
verification.observed: A reviewer can distinguish observed change, built capability, concept, and still-unproven evidence; all three cases expose status in the opening and end with a valid continuation path.

verification.method: Rendered hierarchy review at distance and detail
verification.observed: Measured career impact leads; current project claims remain secondary; detailed evidence is deferred to case pages and artifact captions sit beside the decisions they document.

verification.method: Semantic DOM inspection, CSS contrast calculation, and responsive review
verification.observed: Headings, lists, figures, definition lists, landmarks, and descriptive links are present. Muted text now measures approximately 4.92:1 against the background. Reveal and global CSS contain reduced-motion handling. No assistive-technology session was run.
verification.remediation: Run screen-reader and full keyboard traversal if formal accessibility conformance is required.
verification.settings: Small text, 320px reflow, reduced-motion code path

verification.observed: No browser warnings or errors were reported for the reviewed tab.
verification.settings: Homepage, FORGE, and SBIR Radar during responsive review

verification.artifact: Homepage and all three case-study openings
verification.method: Rendered comparison against the owned commitment and refusal set
verification.observed: Measured career outcomes lead the homepage; current work is explicitly labelled as delivered concept work, a live product with validation ongoing, or a prototype. No visual treatment presents those evidence levels as equivalent.

verification.method: Semantic DOM inspection, direct route loading, and production route manifest
verification.observed: Each homepage work link points to a generated case route; each case exposes a next-case link plus resume, email, and all-work exits; all referenced internal routes are present in the successful static build.
verification.settings: Local browser and Next.js production build

verification.method: State-matrix review, rendered 404 inspection, and compiled recovery-component inspection
verification.observed: Unknown routes render the branded 404 with a home recovery path; SBIR omits an empty product-link group; image dimensions and alternatives preserve delayed/failure meaning; global error provides retry and home escape; non-applicable form, permission, and destructive states are explicitly excluded.
verification.settings: Unknown route, case with no external product link, delayed images, mobile, and reduced motion

verification.method: Rendered long-form content and DOM measurement
verification.observed: Long FORGE and SBIR strings wrap without clipping; the document remains exactly viewport width at 390px and 320px; horizontal phone galleries remain isolated inside their scroll container.
verification.settings: Longest status, outcome, narrative, evidence labels, 390px and 320px viewports

verification.method: Viewport override, screenshots, and layout measurement
verification.observed: Desktop asymmetry collapses to one-column priority order; status moves into the identity block on small screens; proof rows stack; page scrollWidth matches viewport width; touch-oriented links retain 44px minimum height.

verification.method: Scoped semantic, contrast, reflow, focus-style, text-alternative, and reduced-motion inspection
verification.observed: Headings and landmarks are ordered; figures, lists, definition lists, and links expose semantic names; all artifact images have descriptive alternatives; muted small text is approximately 4.92:1; focus-visible styles and a skip link exist; reduced motion disables Reveal entrances. This is a scoped implementation pass, not a claim of formal accessibility conformance.
verification.settings: DOM snapshots, 320px reflow, CSS token calculation, prefers-reduced-motion implementation

verification.observed: First pass established the evidence ledger and interleaved artifacts. Second pass moved status into the case identity block and simplified the proof strip, improving mobile priority and desktop measure without adding ornamental UI.
verification.settings: Distance review, detail review, desktop, 390px, and 320px

verification.observed: The section contains exactly three user/conversion outcomes: 300% daily active user growth, 20% faster maintenance initiation, and roughly 40% platform adoption growth. The five-program standards claim is absent from this section.
Document client width and scroll width both measured 390px after the impact ledger was reduced to three items; no horizontal overflow was introduced.
